summary: DHH live-blogs his excitement during a Steve Jobs keynote, covering Apple's new software launches including Keynote, iLife, and Final Cut Express. He's openly enthusiastic about Keynote as a PowerPoint killer and impressed by the free upgrades in the iLife suite. The post is a brief, real-time reaction piece that captures the infectious energy of Apple product announcements in the early 2000s. DHH self-awarely acknowledges being caught in Jobs' famous Reality Distortion Field.
key_points: Apple's Keynote positioned as a superior PowerPoint replacement at $99
iLife suite upgrades (iPhoto 2, iMovie 3, iTunes) offered for free, defying critics
Final Cut Express brings pro-level video editing to consumers at $299
DHH openly admits to being swept up in Steve Jobs' Reality Distortion Field
money_quotes: Steve Jobs is launching an onslaught of new applications from Apple to make the mac an even more amazing platform (yes, I'm currently in the Jobs Reality Distortion Field).
And putting all the nay-sayers to shame, all the upgrades besides iDVD will be free!
key_insight: Apple's aggressive software strategy—combining premium new apps with free upgrades—was building a platform ecosystem that made the Mac increasingly compelling for creative users.
